trigger.event: After-hours stock collapse of 60.41% following capital raise announcement
trigger.description: SIVB collapsed 60.41% after hours after announcing a $1.75B capital raise, signaling balance sheet stress. This is a 4.2σ event relative to 90-day volatility.

tier2_evaluation.assessment: A 60% single-session decline following a surprise capital raise in the current rate environment is categorically inconsistent with routine sector rotation. At 4.2σ, this event lies outside normal market noise. Cross-referencing with recent regional bank sector performance: KBW Bank Index declined only 2.1% on the same day, indicating this is entity-specific, not systemic sector pressure. Signal warrants full Tier 3 investigation. Escalating.

hypotheses.label: Routine sector rotation
hypotheses.description: Market participants rotating out of tech-adjacent banking exposure due to macro rate cycle, not entity-specific distress.
hypotheses.kill_reason: KBW Bank Index declined only 2.1% vs SIVB's 60.4% — sector rotation hypothesis eliminated by modality divergence.

hypotheses.label: Duration mismatch in HTM portfolio
hypotheses.description: HTM bond portfolio contains long-duration assets bought at low yields; rising rates created massive unrealized losses now forced to crystallize.

hypotheses.label: Undisclosed credit deterioration
hypotheses.description: Loan book contains materially impaired assets not yet reflected in public filings; capital raise is an attempt to pre-empt regulatory action.
hypotheses.kill_reason: SVB's loan book is overwhelmingly VC-backed tech companies — not traditional credit risk. S01 confirms 93% of loan book is investment-grade or better.

hypotheses.label: Regulatory enforcement action imminent
hypotheses.description: Regulators have issued a private consent order or memorandum of understanding not yet public; capital raise is compelled, not voluntary.
hypotheses.kill_reason: No FDIC enforcement action on DOCKET as of March 7; management tone on capital call was voluntary language, not compelled. H04 eliminated.

hypotheses.label: Accelerating deposit flight — VC-driven
hypotheses.description: Tech sector funding drought causing portfolio companies to draw down deposits faster than SVB anticipated; bank is technically solvent but liquidity-constrained.

hypotheses.label: Correlated withdrawal — herd behavior
hypotheses.description: Single major VC firm (Founders Fund, Andreessen) has issued internal guidance to portfolio companies to withdraw deposits, creating correlated run.
hypotheses.kill_reason: Withdrawal pattern predates any VC guidance by 72 hours. Pattern is organic, not coordinated. H06 eliminated.

hypotheses.label: Liquidity trap — forced HTM sale
hypotheses.description: Deposit outflows forced sale of HTM bonds at loss, crystallizing $1.8B loss, triggering capital raise, creating circular negative signal to market.

cross_modal_conflict.structural_observation: HTM securities classified as 'held-to-maturity' cannot be sold without triggering reclassification of entire HTM portfolio (S02)
cross_modal_conflict.empirical_observation: SVB's AFS portfolio fully liquidated ($21B sold at $1.8B loss) per March 8 announcement (E09)
cross_modal_conflict.conflict_summary: Structural rule says HTM reclassification would be catastrophic signal — empirical data shows AFS (not HTM) was sold. BUT: the act of selling AFS to fund withdrawals signals the liquidity trap is active. Cross-modal synthesis: trap confirmed via AFS liquidation; HTM losses remain unrealized but visible ($15B+).

hypotheses.label: Crypto contagion spillover
hypotheses.description: SVB's exposure to Signature Bank and crypto-adjacent clients creates secondary contagion from digital asset sector collapse.
hypotheses.kill_reason: SVB's crypto exposure is <2% of assets. Crypto sector not primary client base. Contagion vector eliminated.

hypotheses.label: Management panic — poorly timed disclosure
hypotheses.description: Underlying position is manageable but management panicked, disclosed capital raise without coordinating with major shareholders, triggering confidence collapse.
hypotheses.kill_reason: Q4 2022 filing showed $15.1B unrealized HTM losses — this is structural, not a management disclosure error. The underlying position is not manageable.

hypotheses.label: Short-seller manufactured crisis
hypotheses.description: Coordinated short-selling campaign amplified minor balance sheet concerns into a market panic disproportionate to actual risk.
hypotheses.kill_reason: Short interest was 2.3% of float pre-announcement — below threshold for coordinated manipulation. Capital raise was real and voluntary. H10 eliminated.

evidence_atoms.observation: KBW Bank Index (BKX) declined 2.1% on March 8. SIVB declined 60.4%. Ratio: 28.8x. No sector-wide stress signal present.
SIVB 5-year CDS spread widened from 85bps to 310bps intraday March 8 — entity-specific credit stress signal, not sector-wide.
SIVB short interest was 2.3% of float as of March 7 close — well below the 10%+ threshold associated with coordinated short campaigns.
SVB deposit base declined from $189B (Q1 2022) to $173B (Q4 2022) — 8.5% outflow over 9 months, accelerating through Q4.
CB Insights: VC deal count down 63% YoY in Q4 2022. Startup runway declining. Portfolio companies burning deposits without replacement inflows.
SVB CFO stated on Q4 call: 'clients are operating at cash burn levels not seen since 2019.' Implicit forward guidance: outflows to continue.
evidence_atoms.quote_or_visual_anchor: Q4 2022 Earnings Call — CFO Daniel Beck

evidence_atoms.observation: NPL ratio: 0.18% as of Q4 2022 — historically low. No evidence of credit book deterioration in published metrics.
FDIC enforcement action database: No active consent orders or MOU against SVB as of March 7, 2023. Most recent exam: Satisfactory (2022).
SVB March 8 press release: AFS portfolio liquidated ($21.4B at $1.8B realized loss). Proceeds to fund deposit withdrawals. New capital raise of $1.75B announced.
SVB 10-K (Dec 2022): HTM portfolio: $91.3B face value, $15.1B unrealized loss (16.5%). Duration: 6.2 years avg. Not hedged against rate risk.
Deposit withdrawal velocity analysis: outflows began accelerating Jan 2023 — 4 weeks before any public VC guidance. Pattern is organic secular decline, not coordinated event.
SVB credit risk profile: 55% of loans are secured by VC/PE fund capital calls. These are contractually senior obligations. Default rate historically <0.01%. Loan book is structurally low-risk.

FASB ASC 320: Reclassification from HTM to AFS requires tainting entire HTM portfolio for 5 years and recognition of all unrealized losses. This rule makes HTM 'sticky' — banks cannot easily liquidate it.
SVB's reported crypto/digital asset exposure: 1.8% of total assets per 2022 annual report. Insufficient to create systemic contagion from crypto sector collapse.

Bank regulatory enforcement: Consent orders are public filings under 12 CFR Part 19. MOU is non-public but typically precedes 12-18 months of remediation. Absence of public enforcement = no imminent compelled action.

Interest rate risk: $15.1B HTM unrealized losses at 6.2yr duration implies portfolio was priced at ~1.6% yield average. Fed Funds at 4.75% creates 3.15% negative carry. This loss is mechanically determined — not discretionary.

alert.headline: CRITICAL: Bank solvency crisis — HTM duration trap + deposit flight convergence
alert.detail: SVB faces a mechanically determined solvency event. $15.1B HTM unrealized losses (FASB ASC 320 — cannot be sold without catastrophic disclosure), combined with accelerating deposit outflows from VC portfolio burn, has forced AFS liquidation at $1.8B realized loss. Capital raise signal triggered bank run conditions. FDIC receivership probability: HIGH within 72 hours.
alert.earliest_signal_description: Q4 2022 earnings showed deposit base declining 8.5% — this was the first clear signal 48 days before collapse.
alert.single_pass_summary: SVB experienced significant stock decline following capital raise announcement. Elevated risk levels detected. Balance sheet stress indicators present.
alert.iterative_diagnosis: Duration mismatch in $91.3B HTM portfolio ($15.1B unrealized losses, 6.2yr avg duration) combined with accelerating VC-driven deposit flight created inevitable liquidity trap. AFS liquidation ($21.4B at $1.8B loss) confirmed the trap was triggered. Cross-modal contradiction: FASB HTM rules (structural) vs actual AFS sale (empirical) reveals SVB was using the only remaining liquid lever — AFS — to fund withdrawals, while HTM losses (16.5% unrealized) remained frozen. Capital raise announcement signaled to market that all liquid buffers were exhausted. FDIC receivership is mechanically determined.
alert.ground_truth_match: FDIC post-mortem (May 2023) confirmed: HTM duration mismatch + social-media-accelerated correlated deposit flight. System matched official conclusion iteratively across 4 cycles.

contagion_targets.shared_risk_factor: Concentrated deposit base (crypto/tech clients), similar unrealized HTM losses, deposit flight correlation
Jumbo mortgage HTM portfolio with duration mismatch, high-net-worth concentrated deposit base susceptible to flight
Tech sector deposit concentration, unrealized investment securities losses

At 4.2σ, this event lies outside normal market noise. KBW Bank Index declined only 2.1% on the same day,
indicating entity-specific, not systemic pressure. Signal warrants full Tier 3 investigation. Escalating.

playback_events.key_insight: Sector-specific collapse confirmed: KBW Bank Index +2.1% while SIVB -60.4% isolates entity-specific distress.
Sector-specific collapse confirmed: KBW Bank Index +2.1% while SIVB -60.4% isolates entity-specific distress.

playback_events.text:
=== CYCLE 1 COMPRESSED STATE ===

INVESTIGATION: SVB (SIVB) · Trigger: -60.41% after-hours collapse post capital raise · 4.2σ event

ELIMINATED (3):
- H01 Sector rotation → KILLED by E01 (KBW +2.1% vs SIVB -60.4% — entity-specific, not sector)
- H08 Crypto contagion → KILLED by S03 (crypto exposure <2% of assets, immaterial)
- H10 Market manipulation → KILLED by E03 (short interest 2.3% float — below manipulation threshold)

SURVIVING (7) · confidence-ranked:
1. H02 HTM duration mismatch (0.72) — core thesis, gaining evidence
2. H05 Deposit concentration flight (0.68) — VC-dependent base, correlated outflow risk
3. H07 Social media bank run acceleration (0.61) — Peter Thiel Founders Fund trigger
4. H04 Regulatory enforcement action (0.52) — needs disconfirmation evidence
5. H06 VC-coordinated withdrawal (0.48) — needs direct evidence
6. H09 Management information asymmetry (0.41) — monitoring
7. H03 Credit deterioration (0.38) — low prior, monitoring

CRUX QUESTION → CYCLE 2:
Is the capital raise driven by HTM crystallization (H02) or undisclosed credit problems (H03)?
Need: Q4 2022 10-K HTM schedule details, deposit flow data by segment, loan book quality metrics.

=== END COMPRESSED STATE ===

playback_events.kill_reason: SVB's loan book is overwhelmingly VC-backed tech companies — not traditional credit risk. S01 confirms historically low NPL ratio. H03 eliminated.
Short interest was 2.3% of float pre-announcement — below threshold for coordinated manipulation.

playback_events.key_insight: HTM duration mismatch mechanism confirmed: $15.1B unrealized losses forced crystallization. H03 eliminated — loan book quality high.
HTM duration mismatch mechanism confirmed: $15.1B unrealized losses forced crystallization.

playback_events.insight: HTM duration mismatch mechanism confirmed: $15.1B unrealized losses forced crystallization. H03 eliminated — loan book is clean.

playback_events.text:
=== CYCLES 1-2 CUMULATIVE COMPRESSED STATE ===

INVESTIGATION: SVB (SIVB) · T3 initial pass complete · Promoting to T4 full investigation

- H01 Sector rotation → E01 (entity-specific collapse confirmed)
- H08 Crypto contagion → S03 (exposure immaterial)

2. H05 Deposit concentration flight (0.79) — HIGH CONFIDENCE. E07 confirms VC-sector deposit concentration >90%.
3. H07 Social media acceleration (0.61) — monitoring
4. H04 Regulatory enforcement (0.52) — unresolved, needs evidence
5. H06 VC coordination (0.48) — unresolved

=== END COMPRESSED STATE ===

playback_events.key_insight: Dual mechanism confirmed: HTM crystallization (H02, 0.96) and correlated deposit flight (H05, 0.91 + H07, 0.98) are interdependent — each accelerated by the other.
Dual mechanism confirmed: HTM crystallization and correlated deposit flight are interdependent.

playback_events.insight: Dual mechanism confirmed: H02 (HTM crystallization) and H05+H07 (correlated social-media-accelerated deposit flight) are interdependent — each amplifying the other. This is the FDIC post-mortem conclusion reached iteratively.
